meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Fix No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to master version control as a student. Use Claude Code and Antigravity to secure your projects and build a professional portfolio with confidence.
content.answer_block: Version control is the bedrock of professional development. By integrating Claude Code with Git, you automate commit history and safeguard your progress. This tutorial guides you through setting up a structured workflow that transforms chaotic codebases into professional, version-tracked repositories, ensuring you never lose a single line of work.
content.intro:
You stare at a folder of scattered files, terrified that a single 'delete' key press could wipe out weeks of late-night progress. The absence of version control isn't just a technical oversight; it’s a barrier between you and a professional career. Every senior developer remembers the sting of lost work, but you don't have to learn that lesson the hard way.

Today, we bridge the gap. We are deploying Claude Code to act as your autonomous engineering assistant, enforcing version control standards from day one. You aren't just writing code; you are building a resilient, industry-standard development pipeline that proves your competence to future employers.

steps.title: Initialize the Repository
steps.description: Establish a secure foundation for your project by initializing Git, ensuring every change is tracked from the first character.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project structure and initialize a local Git repository with a standard .gitignore for modern web development.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Auth
steps.description: Connect your development environment to the Anthropic API to allow Claude to execute terminal commands safely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify my environment variables and ensure the Claude CLI has the necessary permissions to access my local file system.

steps.title: Automate Commit Messages
steps.description: Use Claude to generate context-aware, semantic commit messages that tell a story about your code changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current staged changes and generate a semantic commit message following conventional commit standards.

steps.title: Establish a Branching Workflow
steps.description: Learn to isolate features using branches, preventing experimental code from breaking your stable 'main' branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new feature branch named 'feature/auth-setup' and switch to it, ensuring the working directory is clean.

steps.title: Perform Automated Diffs
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze your changes before merging, catching bugs before they enter your production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the changes in the current branch compared to main and identify any potential regressions or logic errors.

pro_tips: Never commit API keys or environment secrets; add them to your .gitignore immediately upon creation.
Use semantic versioning (v1.0.0) tags on your GitHub releases to track your project milestones for your portfolio.
Configure Claude to automatically run linting tests before every commit to ensure your code quality remains high.
Commit early and often; atomic commits make it significantly easier to revert specific features if something breaks.
Use 'git log --graph' to visualize your project history and understand how your features connect over time.
content.next_steps: Once you have mastered local version control, push your local repository to a remote platform like GitHub and document your progress in a README.md file to showcase your journey to recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build software like a professional. Stop worrying about lost files and start focusing on shipping features that matter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
